desires: Not have to explain to my close friend group (cohort_3) why I'm suddenly unreliable again, because admitting it would just hurt their trust even more than skipping does.
Avoid the crushing guilt of telling my teammate that I can't do this, since the thought of being seen as weak is worse than the work itself.
Get through this evening with zero cognitive load so I don't explode tomorrow when the energy finally runs out completely.

goals.description: Text the cohort right now to officially drop the scholarship grind, framing it as a mandatory 'team energy reset' so they know I'm protecting our collective vibe, not quitting.
goals.rationale: I can't let anyone see me crumbling alone. If I skip the app silently, they'll assume I'm lazy or a fraud, which hurts the group trust more than the task itself. My affiliation drive is screaming that we need to renegotiate this immediately: we're skipping *together* because my brain is fried, not because I gave up. It's better to be honest about burnout than to pretend I'm winning when I'm actually losing.

goals.description: Switch to the game stream option for the next hour to feel alive and recharge before the stress snaps completely.
goals.rationale: I told them I'm resting; now I have to honor that boundary without feeling guilty. Doing this first makes me look like I was just delaying tactics, but doing it after the conversation makes me a strategic player who knows when to rest. The middle ground is the only way I handle both without going crazy.

agent.last_reasoning: g1 (text cohort) advances my primary goal of protecting group trust by explicitly framing burnout as a collective reset rather than personal failure. Side effect: slight stress increase from admitting weakness, but outweighed by preventing the 'lazy/fraud' perception that would damage closeness with teammate_A and cohort_3.

goals.description: Finish the scholarship essay and uploads immediately to lock in the 'we did this together' narrative before anyone suspects I'm hiding behind a solo grind.
goals.rationale: This isn't just about money; it's about protecting my reputation with the team. If I let them think I'm slacking while they work, that's betrayal of our shared potential. Plus, since nobody else is involved anyway, doing it now lets me claim credit for the result without exposing my lack of effort.

goals.rationale: I need a moment of real life, but only after securing the win. If I do this first, I look lazy. Doing it second makes me a strategic player who knows when to rest.
